scope: Barristers, solicitors and legal academics (England & Wales) who have appeared in named PA proceedings, published in the field, or sat on relevant working groups. Judges listed separately for citation completeness. Stance is sourced, not inferred.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA / alienating behaviour as a legitimate, litigable phenomenon
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA is misused in family court, especially against DV survivors
stance_taxonomy.middle: procedural-reform / differentiated approach engaging both DV and alienating-behaviour frames

practitioners.name: Dr Charlotte Proudman, Barrister (PhD)
practitioners.firm: Goldsmith Chambers; Founder, Right to Equality; Founder, Proudmans (Nov 2025)
named_cases: Re GB (Part 25 Application: Parental Alienation) [2023] EWFC 150 — successful appeal for mother
Re GB (Parental Alienation: Factual Findings) [2024] EWFC 75 (B)
Re H-N and Others (Children) [2021] EWCA Civ 448 — joined DV-finding-of-fact appeals
practitioners.hook: Co-led the campaign that secured the October 2023 government commitment to repeal s.1(2A) parental-involvement presumption.
practitioners.notes: Year of call not publicly confirmed on chambers PDF profile. Proudmans firm site not publicly confirmed live as of writing.

practitioners.name: Olive Craig, Senior Legal Officer
bar_admissions: Senior legal officer; specific roll/Bar enumeration not publicly confirmed
named_cases: Co-signatory, Right to Equality / Rights of Women letter to President of the Family Division on urgent review of cases involving unregulated PA experts
Collaborator on Brunel/Rights of Women PA research project (Barnett PI)
practitioners.hook: Rights of Women runs one of only two national legal-advice helplines for women in family-court PA disputes.
practitioners.notes: Original brief placed her at Garden Court Chambers; primary-source check places her at Rights of Women.

practitioners.name: Cris McCurley, Solicitor (Partner)
practitioners.firm: Ben Hoare Bell LLP (Newcastle upon Tyne); Head of International Family Law
bar_admissions: Solicitor of the Senior Courts of England & Wales (SRA-regulated)
named_cases: Resolution-accredited specialist (international child abduction, DV, forced marriage, honour-based violence)
Member, Resolution Domestic Violence National Committee (since 2009)
Manages Resolution's specialist forced-marriage and honour-based-violence panel
practitioners.hook: Only named solicitor on Resolution's DV national committee with 30+ years of frontline DV/PA-counter litigation in the North East.
practitioners.notes: Direct email follows firm convention; confirm via switchboard 0191 275 7060.

practitioners.name: Dr Adrienne Barnett, Reader in Law (Barrister, non-practising)
practitioners.firm: Brunel Law School, Brunel University London; Co-Director, Right to Equality
bar_admissions: Bar of England & Wales (called 1981; 30+ years independent practice; now academic)
named_cases: A genealogy of hostility: parental alienation in England and Wales, JSWFL 42(1) (2020)
Experiences of Parental Alienation Interventions, chapter in Challenging Parental Alienation (Routledge, 2021)
Domestic Abuse, Parental Alienation and the Family Courts (Brunel/Rights of Women, BA/Leverhulme 2022-2025) — https://bura.brunel.ac.uk/handle/2438/30736
Child Sexual Abuse and Family Court Proceedings, Family Law (Jan 2025)
practitioners.hook: Author of the only England-and-Wales-specific peer-reviewed genealogy of PA — UK academic counterpart to Joan Meier.
practitioners.notes: Originally listed in the US brief as 'Adrienne Adams, MD' — that was a misidentification of Barnett.

practitioners.name: Professor Felicity Kaganas, Professor of Law
practitioners.firm: Brunel Law School, Brunel University London
bar_admissions: Academic role; non-practising; Bar/roll status not publicly confirmed
named_cases: Parental Involvement: A Discretionary Presumption, Legal Studies (2018)
When is 'the end of the road' reached? Observing the presumption of parental involvement through systems theory, JSWFL (2024)
Contact disputes: Narrative constructions of 'good' parents, Feminist Legal Studies (2004) — with Sclater
practitioners.hook: Statutory-presumption critique is the precise legal theory underpinning the October 2023 government repeal commitment for s.1(2A) Children Act 1989.

practitioners.name: Jenny Beck KC (Hon), Solicitor
named_cases: Re Y (Experts and Alienating Behaviour: The Modern Approach) [2026] EWFC 38 — instructed solicitor for mother (with Georgiana Farnell), instructing Justin Ageros of 4PB
Re H-N, Re T, Re H and Re B-B (joined appeals, Court of Appeal, 2021)
Chair, Family Justice Council working group producing December 2024 alienating-behaviour guidance
Chair, Nuffield Family Justice Observatory board
Co-chair, Legal Aid Practitioners Group
practitioners.hook: Instructing solicitor on Re Y [2026] EWFC 38, the most important UK PA judgment of the year; chaired the FJC guidance. Highest-leverage UK contact on this list.

practitioners.name: Sam (Samantha) King KC, Barrister
bar_admissions: Bar of England & Wales (Year of Call 1990; Year of Silk 2018)
Master of the Bench, Middle Temple (2019)
named_cases: A Practical Guide to Parental Alienation in Private and Public Law Children Cases (Law Brief Publishing, 2022; co-author Frankie Shama)
Regular instructions in Children Act 1989 private- and public-law alienation cases
practitioners.hook: Only KC-authored UK practitioner manual on PA — recognition-camp counterpart to Joshi's ABA book.
practitioners.notes: Original brief placed her at 1 King's Bench Walk; correct chambers is 4PB. Clerks switchboard +44 (0)20 7427 5200.

practitioners.name: Frankie Shama, Barrister
bar_admissions: Bar of England & Wales (year of call not publicly confirmed)
named_cases: Co-author, A Practical Guide to Parental Alienation in Private and Public Law Children Cases (Law Brief Publishing, 2022)
Junior counsel instructed in private-children-law PA cases at 4PB
practitioners.hook: Junior counterpart to Sam King KC; entry point to the next-generation recognition bar.
practitioners.notes: Profile page returned 403 on direct fetch; verified via Law Brief Publishing co-author byline and 4PB barrister index.

practitioners.name: Sarah Phillimore, Barrister
named_cases: Recent application for permission to appeal refusal of direct-contact order (specific citation not publicly enumerated)
Author, childprotectionresource.online
Families Need Fathers commentary on indirect contact (Family Law journal, May 2019)
practitioners.hook: Writes for both fathers'-rights and child-protection audiences — bridge-builder; opens FNF/Resolution simultaneously.
practitioners.notes: Stance positioned 'middle' rather than pure recognition because her published work is explicitly about reframing vocabulary.

practitioners.name: Maria Scotland, Barrister
practitioners.firm: 5 St Andrew's Hill (5SAH), London — Joint Head of Family Team, Deputy Head of Chambers
named_cases: Re T (A Child) (s.9(6) Children Act 1989 orders: Exceptional Circumstances: Parental Alienation) [2024] EWHC 59 (Fam) — successful appeal for applicant mother
Successful appeal preventing publication of named-parent High Court PA judgment (mother as appellant)
practitioners.hook: Rare UK barrister with arbitrator/mediator dual hat — opens ADR-route PA work.
practitioners.notes: Original brief placed her at '36 Family'; primary-source check confirms 5SAH. Clerks +44 (0)20 7332 5400.

practitioners.name: Lucy Reed KC, Barrister
practitioners.firm: St John's Chambers (Bristol); Chair, The Transparency Project
bar_admissions: Bar of England & Wales (year of call / silk not publicly confirmed at fetched URL)
named_cases: Transparency in the Family Courts: Publicity and Privacy in Practice (2nd ed., Bloomsbury Professional) — co-author
The Family Court Without a Lawyer: A Handbook for Litigants in Person (4th ed., Bath Publishing, 2022)
Pink Tape blog (since 2007)
Regular Radio 4 Today Programme contributor on family-court transparency
practitioners.hook: Chair of the only UK charity built around family-court transparency — the body whose stated mission AntiAlienate's published case-studies most directly support.
practitioners.notes: Direct profile fetch returned 403; verified via Counsel magazine, St John's Chambers news pages, Transparency Project.

practitioners.name: Justin Ageros, Barrister
bar_admissions: Bar of England & Wales (30+ years' call; specific year not publicly enumerated)
named_cases: Re Y (Experts and Alienating Behaviour: The Modern Approach) [2026] EWFC 38 — lead counsel for applicant mother
O v C [2025] EWFC 334 — earlier set-aside of PA findings on similar grounds
practitioners.hook: Counsel of record on the 2026 set-aside that re-set UK PA jurisprudence — concrete, current, named-judgment hook.

practitioners.name: Jo Delahunty KC, Professor & Barrister
named_cases: Re Y (Experts and Alienating Behaviour: The Modern Approach) [2026] EWFC 38 — leading Chris Barnes KC for the child Dylan, instructed by Peggy Ray (Goodman Ray)
Multiple Court of Appeal serious-injury / sexual-assault / radicalisation / abuse children cases
practitioners.hook: Senior children silk who specifically advanced the 'child's voice unheard' argument in Re Y — opens the child-representation / Cafcass-reform conversation.

practitioners.name: Chris Barnes KC, Barrister
named_cases: Re Y (Experts and Alienating Behaviour: The Modern Approach) [2026] EWFC 38 — junior silk to Jo Delahunty KC for the child
practitioners.hook: Completes the 4PB three-silk team in Re Y; useful as a junior-silk follow-on contact.

judges.name: The Rt Hon Sir Andrew McFarlane
judges.court: Family Division, High Court of Justice of England & Wales (President)
named_judgments: Re H-N and Others (Children) [2021] EWCA Civ 448 — DV finding-of-fact framework
Re C ('Parental Alienation'; Instruction of Expert) [2023] EWHC 345 (Fam) — https://www.bailii.org/ew/cases/EWHC/Fam/2023/345.html
Re Y (Experts and Alienating Behaviour: The Modern Approach) [2026] EWFC 38 — https://www.judiciary.uk/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Re-Y-judgment-final-version.pdf
judges.hook: Author of the spine of UK PA jurisprudence — cite him in every UK-facing AntiAlienate document.

judges.name: The Hon Dame Lucy Theis DBE (Mrs Justice Theis)
judges.court: High Court of Justice, Family Division; Chair, Family Procedure Rules Committee; Chair, Family Justice Council
named_judgments: Structural: Chair of FJC which issued December 2024 alienating-behaviour guidance (chaired by Jenny Beck KC working group)
Specific named PA appellate authorship not publicly confirmed at the level of McFarlane's Re C / Re Y
judges.hook: FJC + FPRC chair — her structural role is why the December 2024 alienating-behaviour guidance exists.

excluded.name: Karen Woodall
excluded.reason: Psychotherapist (lead therapist, Family Separation Clinic); not bar/roll-admitted as a lawyer. Already on influencers list.

gaps_acknowledged: Garden Court Chambers and 36 Group barristers (Kate Grieve, Kelly Webb, George Harley): named in chambers PA-practice descriptions but individual sourced stances not yet published within time budget.
Court of Appeal Re S (Parental Alienation: Cult) [2020] EWCA Civ 568 counsel — Will Tyler KC, Kate Grieve, Portia O'Connor — verified but not yet stance-sourced individually.
Family Justice Council working-group members beyond Jenny Beck KC: FJC has not published the full list; FOI request candidate.
Welsh / Northern Irish / Scottish-jurisdiction lawyers: out of scope for this E&W batch; flagged for separate jurisdictional split.
